creator: des@flood.ping.uio.no
text:
Makoto MATSUSHITA <matusita@jp.freebsd.org> writes:
> fetch(1) bundled with FreeBSD-current as of July/11/2000 (yes, today)
> is somewhat broken. It cannot fetch the *original* tgzball of thttpd,
> http://www.acme.com/software/thttpd/thttpd-2.19.tar.gz. Here is a
> sample session:
> 
> % fetch -A http://www.acme.com/software/thttpd/thttpd-2.19.tar.gz
> fetch: thttpd-2.19.tar.gz: size not known
> fetch: Not Found
> % 

The server reports a 404:

des@md5 ~% fetch -A http://www.acme.com/software/thttpd/thttpd-2.19.tar.gz
scheme:   [http]
user:     []
password: []
host:     [www.acme.com]
port:     [0]
document: [/software/thttpd/thttpd-2.19.tar.gz]
---> www.acme.com:80
>>> HEAD http://www.acme.com:80/software/thttpd/thttpd-2.19.tar.gz HTTP/1.1
>>> Host: www.acme.com:80
>>> User-Agent: fetch libfetch/2.0
>>> Connection: close

response: [HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found]
code:     [404]
fetch: thttpd-2.19.tar.gz: size not known
---> www.acme.com:80
>>> GET http://www.acme.com:80/software/thttpd/thttpd-2.19.tar.gz HTTP/1.1
>>> Host: www.acme.com:80
>>> User-Agent: fetch libfetch/2.0
>>> Connection: close

response: [HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found]
code:     [404]
fetch: Not Found

(to obtain this output, rebuild libfetch with DEBUG defined)

This is caused by a bug in Apache, which is tickled by a misfeature of
the http code in libfetch. Libfetch always sends absolute URIs in HTTP
requests, though according to RFC2068 it should only do so when
connected to a proxy server; however, RFC2068 also requires servers to
accept absolute URIs even though clients aren't supposed to send them.
Apache sometimes does and sometimes doesn't, and I haven't been able
to determine exactly what causes it to reject them. Fixing libfetch to
only use absolute URIs when talking to a proxy isn't trivial, because
the part of the code that sends the request doesn't know whether or
not it's connected to a proxy, but there are so many buggy servers out
there I guess there's no way around. I'll try to fix it tonight.

DES
-- 
Dag-Erling Smorgrav - des@flood.ping.uio.no
